title: Buy a Cheap Nissan Note UK | Common Issues & Buyer Tips | BuyAndScrap
metaDescription: Looking for a cheap Nissan Note UK? Discover common issues, what to check before buying, and top tips for finding a great deal at BuyAndScrap.com!
content:
The Nissan Note is a fantastic choice if you’re looking for a **cheap Nissan Note UK**. This compact MPV offers surprising space, great fuel economy, and is easy to drive. To find a reliable used model, always check for common issues like rust, suspension wear, and proper function of the CVT gearbox. Follow our honest advice to secure a brilliant bargain from BuyAndScrap.com.

## Why the Nissan Note is a Savvy Choice for UK Drivers

Before diving into checks, let's understand why the Nissan Note is so popular. It offers an excellent blend of practicality and low running costs. This makes it a firm favourite for city living, small families, or anyone needing extra boot space.

### Practicality & Interior Space

Don't let its size fool you. The Note boasts a clever design, providing generous headroom and legroom for all passengers. Many models feature a sliding rear bench. This lets you choose between more passenger comfort or increased boot capacity. It’s genuinely versatile for everyday UK life.

### Excellent Fuel Economy

Whether you pick a petrol or diesel engine, the Nissan Note is known for being efficient. Smaller petrol engines are great for city commutes, offering good MPG. Diesel versions shine on longer journeys, delivering impressive fuel figures. This helps keep your running costs low.

### Easy to Drive

The Note is a breeze to manoeuvre, especially on busy UK roads. It has light steering, great all-round visibility, and compact dimensions. New drivers will find it confidence-inspiring. Experienced drivers will appreciate its stress-free nature in tight spots.

## What to Check When Buying a **Cheap Nissan Note UK**: Our Expert Tips

Even reliable cars have weak points. When searching for a **cheap Nissan Note UK**, knowing what to inspect can save you money and headaches. Our team at BuyAndScrap have seen thousands of cars. Here's what we advise looking for.

### 1. Rust: A Common UK Car Foe

Rust is a major concern, especially on older, first-generation models (2006-2013). Check the rear wheel arches, sills, and around the boot lid carefully. Look under the car too, paying attention to the subframe and suspension mounting points. Minor surface rust on an older car might be acceptable, but widespread or structural rust is a deal-breaker. Always walk away from anything serious.

### 2. Suspension & Steering Health

Listen for any clunks, knocks, or creaks over bumps during your test drive. Worn suspension bushes, anti-roll bar linkages, or shock absorbers are common wear items. These aren't usually very expensive to fix. However, they can indicate how well the car has been maintained. Uneven tyre wear can also suggest suspension issues.

### 3. CVT Gearbox (Automatic Models Only)

Many automatic Notes use a Continuously Variable Transmission (CVT). If serviced correctly, these are usually reliable. However, high mileage or poor maintenance can cause problems. Listen for excessive whining noises during acceleration. The car should accelerate smoothly without hesitation or jerking. A proper test drive at various speeds is crucial here. If it feels hesitant, get it checked by a mechanic.

### 4. Service History & MOT Records

A complete service history is gold, even for a cheap car. It shows the previous owner cared for the vehicle. Check the MOT history online using our [Free MOT Checker](/mot-checker). Look for advisory notes that haven’t been addressed, or repeated failures on the same items. This reveals potential ongoing issues.

### 5. Electrics & Interior Condition

Test all electrics: windows, mirrors, air conditioning, radio, and dashboard lights. Ensure everything works as it should. Check for damp smells or water ingress, especially in the boot or under the carpets. A worn interior isn't necessarily a bad sign on a cheap car. However, major damage might point to neglect.

### 6. Tyres, Brakes & Lights

Inspect all four tyres for even wear and sufficient tread depth. UK law requires at least 1.6mm across the central three-quarters of the tyre. Look for cracks or bulges. Check the brake discs and pads for excessive wear or scoring. Finally, ensure all exterior lights (headlights, indicators, brake lights) are working correctly.

## General Buying Advice for a Used Car

Finding a **cheap Nissan Note UK** also means following some universal advice for buying used cars.

*   **View in Daylight:** Always inspect the car in natural light. This helps you spot paint discrepancies, dents, or rust that might be hidden by artificial light.
*   **Test Drive Thoroughly:** Drive on various roads – city, open road, and over speed bumps. Listen for unusual noises and feel how the car handles. Test the brakes firmly.
*   **Get an Independent Inspection:** If you're serious, consider a pre-purchase inspection from a trusted mechanic. It's a small cost that can save you a fortune.
*   **Check Paperwork:** Ensure the V5C (logbook) matches the seller's address and the car's VIN. Verify service records and MOT certificates. Never buy a car without its V5C.
*   **Payment:** Use a secure payment method and get a receipt. Avoid carrying large sums of cash.

## Frequently Asked Questions About the Nissan Note

### Is the Nissan Note a reliable car?
Yes, the Nissan Note is generally considered reliable. Its engines are robust, and it performs well if serviced regularly. Keep an eye on common wear items like suspension components and potential rust on older models.

### What is the best engine for a Nissan Note?
For city driving and fuel economy, the 1.2-litre petrol engine (later models) or the 1.4-litre petrol (earlier models) are excellent. If you do more motorway miles, the 1.5-litre dCi diesel offers impressive MPG. It's about matching the engine to your driving style.

### Are Nissan Note parts expensive in the UK?
Nissan Note parts are generally affordable and widely available in the UK. Many components are shared with other Nissan and Renault models, keeping costs down. Labour costs will vary by garage.

### How much does it cost to tax a Nissan Note in the UK?
Road tax (VED) for a Nissan Note varies. Older models are based on CO2 emissions. Newer 1.2-litre petrol models (after April 2017) often fall into the flat £180 annual rate. Always check the specific car's VED before buying.

Finding a reliable and **cheap Nissan Note UK** doesn’t have to be a gamble. By knowing what to look for and following our advice, you can buy with confidence.
